LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ADAM CRONIN'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. ALTHOUGH DEFENDANT IS CORRECT THAT PLAINTIFF'S MOVING PAPERS ARE DEFICIENT UNDER CAL. R. CT. 3.1324, DEFENDANT DOES NOT SHOW THAT IT WILL BE SUBSTANTIALLY PREJUDICED IF THE AMENDMENT IS ALLOWED. DEFENDANT MAY RAISE ITS LIMITATIONS OR OTHER DEFENSES ON DEMURRER OR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. (SEE, E.G., CALIFORNIA CASUALTY GEN. INS. V. SUPERIOR COURT (1985) 173 CAL.APP.3D 274, 281 ["THE PREFERABLE PRACTICE WOULD BE TO PERMIT THE AMENDMENT AND ALLOW THE PARTIES TO TEST ITS LEGAL SUFFICIENCY BY DEMURRER, MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS OR OTHER APPROPRIATE PROCEEDINGS."].) IF PG&E WISHES TO MOVE FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF THE AMENDED CAUSE OF ACTION AND IS UNABLE TO DO SO TIMELY IN LIGHT OF THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE, IT MAY SEEK LEAVE TO HAVE THE MOTION HEARD LESS THAN 30 DAYS BEFORE TRIAL, OR THE PARTIES MAY STIPULATE TO HAVE IT HEARD ON LESS THAN 75 DAYS STATUTORY NOTICE. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

DEMURRER to 3RD Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-09-2021 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY's DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT.

No appearances. The tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

PG&E's demurrer to the third amended complaint (TAC) is overruled. A "demurrer does not lie as to a portion of a cause of action and if any part of a cause of action is properly pleaded, the demurrer will be overruled." (Elder v. Pacific Bell Telephone Co. (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th 841 n.14.) As PG&E states, the TAC pleads a fourth "reason" for plaintiff's cause of action for wrongful termination in violation of public policy - that he was terminated for filing a complaint in court. This joins three prior reasons, including filing safety complaints. The parties' relation-back arguments need not be reached at this juncture.
Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and complies with CRC 3.1312.

If PG&E wishes to move for summary adjudication of the amended cause of action and is unable to do so timely in light of the current trial date, it may seek leave to have the motion heard less than 30 days before trial, or the parties may stipulate to have it heard on less than 75 days statutory notice.

Defendant Pacific Gas And Electric Company'S Ntice Of Demurrer And Demiurrer To Plaintiff'S Second Amended Complaint (Added Per Judge Schulman'S Oral Order During Ex Parte Hearing Held On 6/30/20)
Off Calendar Jul-21-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Adam Cronin (916-467-6040; ajcu88@gmail.com) plaintiff in propria persona.
Katherine Kimsey (415.677.3168; KKimsey@littler.com) for Defendant, PG&E

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Pacific Gas And Electric Company's Demurrer To Plaintiff'S Second Amended Complaint:

Defendant PG&E's demurrer to the fourth cause of action for wrongful termination (Labor Code 1102.5) is overruled. Plaintiff alleges sufficient facts that defendant terminated him because he was poised to report defendant's safety violations. (See Second Amended Complaint, pars. 88-108; Diego v. Pilgrim United Church of Christ (2014) 231 Cal.App.4th 913, 923-924, 929 [Labor Code 1102.5 claim could be based on discharge where employer believed that plaintiff employee might file a workplace safety complaint].)

The demurrer to the sixth cause of action for intrusion is sustained without leave to amend. "[I]n order to prevail on an intrusion-into-private-matters cause of action, a plaintiff must establish that the defendant intentionally intruded into a private place, conversation, or matter in a manner highly offensive to a reasonable person?The tort is proven only if the plaintiff had an objectively reasonable expectation of seclusion or solitude in the place, conversation, or data source." (Taus v. Loftus (2007) 40 Cal.4th 683, 730.) Here, plaintiff alleges that defendant attended plaintiff's public arraignment and then subsequently interviewed plaintiff's ex-girlfriend. (SAC, pars. 39, 133.) Since there are no allegations that defendant obtained the information through improper means, the claim fails. (See id. at 725-726 [plaintiff could not establish prima facie case of intrusion into private matters based upon defendants' collection and dissemination of information contained in court records open to the public]; id. at 732-733, 735-737 [explaining that simply posing questions is not actionable].)

order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
